sn56.48:1.1: “Monks, suppose that this great earth were totally covered with water,
sn56.48:1.2: and a man were to toss a yoke with a single hole there.
sn56.48:1.3: A wind from the east would push it west; a wind from the west would push it east. A wind from the north would push it south; a wind from the south would push it north.
sn56.48:1.4: And suppose a blind sea turtle were there. It would come to the surface once every one hundred years.
sn56.48:1.6: Would that blind sea turtle, coming to the surface once every one hundred years, stick his neck into the yoke with a single hole?”
sn56.48:1.7: “It would be a sheer coincidence, lord, that the blind sea turtle, coming to the surface once every one hundred years, would stick his neck into the yoke with a single hole.”
sn56.48:2.1: “It’s likewise a sheer coincidence that one obtains the human state.
sn56.48:2.2: It’s likewise a sheer coincidence that a Tathāgata, worthy & rightly self-awakened, arises in the world.
sn56.48:2.3: It’s likewise a sheer coincidence that a Dhamma & Vinaya expounded by a Tathāgata appears in the world.
sn56.48:2.4: Now, this human state has been obtained. A Tathāgata, worthy & rightly self-awakened, has arisen in the world. A Dhamma & Vinaya expounded by a Tathāgata appears in the world.
sn56.48:3.1: “Therefore your duty is the contemplation, ‘This is stress … This is the origination of stress … This is the cessation of stress.’ Your duty is the contemplation, ‘This is the path of practice leading to the cessation of stress.’”
